title: Falls Backwards Into the Void with a Smile — Self-Image Manifesto
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir sketches the self archetype he's chasing: the man who falls backwards into the void grinning, peace sign up, riding it like the time of his life.
  • Battlefield image: leads his crew head-first, can lose an arm, eyes, soul-on-the-verge — "zero wavering, pure soul fire, God."
  • Core contrast move: with family / kids / wife he's "a butterfly… tender… goofy little playmate"; with friends "silly, intense, fun, joyful, spinning, twirling."
  • Closes on agency: live on his own weird terms, "own a cord, hacky and genius… you decide the outcome of things. You bend reality to your will."
  • This is one of the cleanest manifesto style entries in the vault — a self-portrait, not an event log.

Notes

A short, dense self-image note from late February 2026. The structure is three archetypes stacked: (1) the void-faller — the playful surrender to chaos, (2) the war-leader — terminal commitment to people in front of him, (3) the butterfly at home — the soft register reserved for chosen family. The point is not that he wants to be any one of them, it's that he wants the contrast — the same person rendering all three without leakage between them.

Belongs in the career archetypes neighborhood and pairs naturally with be your own original. The closing line — "you bend reality to your will" — is the same posture as max effort commit and disconnected from outcome: act with totality, don't ask permission from outside.

Compare with the wishlist tone of exactly what I want four months earlier — same imaginative move (specify it until it's real), different domain (intimate textures vs. interior posture).

Connections

  • — form-defining example.
  • career archetypes spy leader creator — same archetype-stacking move.
  • be your own original / max effort commit / disconnected from outcome — adjacent self-direction concepts.
  • self as archetype — new concept page this note grounds.
  • 2025 10 28 exactly what i want — sister piece in the imagine-it-specifically tradition.
